title: Cheap deterministic tests are rerun, not archived as experiments
summary: Frontend test outputs are disposable; durable evidence consists of fixtures, assertions, harness code, and explicit behavior records.
details: The full isolated suite costs seconds of ordinary CPU time and can be regenerated on demand.
The repository does not create per-run directories, preserve ordinary logs, hash every runner, or maintain an experiment ledger for routine frontend validation.
Platform-specific or expensive performance observations may be recorded when they cannot be reconstructed cheaply, but that is an exception.
rationale: The thesis repository's experiment ceremony exists because cluster results are expensive and scarce; copying it here would create maintenance cost without preserving scarce evidence.
Committed tests provide stronger and cheaper evidence for this real-time utility.
alternatives: Mirror the thesis repository's QUESTION, ANSWER, out, and LEDGER structure for every validation run; rejected as cost-model cargo culting.
Keep chat transcripts or ad hoc logs as evidence; rejected because they are neither canonical nor needed for rerunnable tests.

provenance.note: Owner distinguished expensive SLURM experiments from cheap real-time frontend tests.

evidence.claim: The complete current suite is cheap enough to rerun routinely.

aliases: do not preserve routine test logs
questions: Should every frontend test run get an experiment directory?
What test evidence is durable in this repository?
Why are ordinary headless outputs disposable?

last_verified.method: Owner ruling plus measured isolated suite cost.
